mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which weakens corporate earnings prospects and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and industrial production indicates robust economic expansion, which boosts corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.05 for any 3-year rolling window when both GDPC1 and INDPRO are rising.

mechanism_narrative: In high inflation environments, central bank tightening to curb price pressures leads to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns exceed 1.5% in 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 235 and FEDFUNDS > 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate strong demand for residential construction, which signals a healthy economy and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in housing boom periods fall below 0.5% in 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing industrial production reflects robust manufacturing activity, which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in INDPRO growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth indicates a tightening labor market, which boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in payroll growth periods fall below 0.3% in 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high consumer spending, as measured by RSAFS, signals strong demand for goods and services, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in RSAFS growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
